presentation_options.text: What is different in meaning between “pam” and “pam”? What does the extra part do?
Starting from “pam” (meaning ‘pam’), how do you say it when it is a different person, number, or tense?
If we change the subject from “pam” to “pam”, does “pam” change? How?
Inside “pam”, is “pam” a piece you can take off and still have a word, or does it only ever appear stuck on?
Is there a difference between “pam” and “pam”? Do they mean different things, or are they the same word said two ways?
Can you say “pam”? Does it sound right / natural to you?
Which of these sounds most natural: ‘pam’? Are any of them wrong?
